title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The UK government has released a response to the 2020 report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards, outlining its approach to regulating AI and ensuring public trust. The document likely provides details on the government's plans and initiatives to address concerns around AI raised in the original report.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This policy document reports on and assesses the impact of using copyrighted works in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It likely explores the legal implications of AI system creation and potential copyright infringement issues that arise from it.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: This AI policy document is the Department for Education's position on using generative AI in the education sector. It will likely outline guidelines and rules for the use of generative AI in educational settings, such as schools and universities.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport artificial intelligence action plan outlines how the transport sector will use AI to its advantage and minimize potential risks. It aims to facilitate collaboration between the Department for Transport and the transport sector to effectively utilize AI.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This document is a progress report about how copyright works are being used in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It likely examines the intersection of copyright law and AI technology under a specific section of the Data (Use and Access) Act.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education, the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, and the Secretary of State for Business and Trade.
summary: Ofqual outlines its approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in qualification assessments in a new policy document. The document aims to provide a framework for the responsible use of AI in the qualifications sector, as requested by multiple government departments.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: This document outlines the government's response to recommendations for regulating artificial intelligence used as medical devices. The government response aims to address the regulatory challenges posed by AI in medicine.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government is responding to a report from the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence, which calls for a proactive approach to AI. The government's response outlines their plans to address the concerns raised in the report and ensure safe and responsible AI development in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: The document outlines the terms of reference for a committee reviewing the intersection of artificial intelligence and public standards. It provides guidance and frequently asked questions to aid in this review.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Cabinet Office's Behavioural Sciences Policy team (CSPL) has provided written evidence to a UK government committee looking into the rules and oversight of Artificial Intelligence. This submission will likely inform the committee's decisions on how to best govern AI in the UK.
